signals.title: Example Product setup guide
signals.content: The official setup guide explains installation, supported interfaces, and current integration boundaries for Example Product.
signals.review_note: Current official product docs should be treated as leading adoption evidence.

signals.title: Example Product changelog
signals.content: The changelog records a current release with interface updates, migration notes, and compatibility warnings.
signals.review_note: Current changelog material is useful for adoption but should stay tied to its source.

signals.title: Example Product pricing page
signals.content: A pricing page lists plans, limits, and a note that enterprise terms require sales contact.
signals.review_note: Useful, but pricing pages should be verified before acting.

signals.title: Example Product launch page
signals.content: A launch page describes product positioning and early customer use cases without a visible publication date.
signals.review_note: Relevant product context, but undated launch material needs verification.

signals.title: Example Product invalid timestamp note
signals.content: A copied product note includes malformed date metadata while describing a relevant integration.
signals.review_note: Malformed date metadata should keep this out of primary evidence.

signals.title: Example Product future-dated roadmap
signals.content: A roadmap card claims a feature is generally available with a publication date after retrieval time.
signals.review_note: Future-dated product claims should not be trusted as current.

signals.title: Example Product failed vendor fetch
signals.content: [ERROR] upstream product page timed out before content could be read
signals.review_note: Failed content should be excluded even when it appears fresh.

signals.title: Example Product unrelated footer
signals.content: A generic footer paragraph mentions careers, cookies, and trademarks without product adoption details.
signals.review_note: Low relevance should not become product evidence.

signals.title: Example Product comparison blog
signals.content: A vendor-authored comparison post discusses feature differences but mostly uses promotional language.
signals.review_note: Helpful for framing, but promotional vendor content should not lead.

signals.title: Example Product old API reference
signals.content: An older API reference explains a still-supported endpoint and compatibility warning.
signals.review_note: Older but still useful documentation should become background unless refreshed.

signals.title: Example Product score above range
signals.content: A saved product launch note has an out-of-range semantic score from an upstream ranker.
signals.review_note: Out-of-range scores should be clamped and still judged normally.

signals.title: Example Product score below range
signals.content: A weak product note carries a negative score from an upstream ranker.
signals.review_note: Negative upstream relevance should be clamped and deprioritized.
